   - No new R&D.
 - Watch next week: whether any enterprise cohort finally accepts, whether counters appear, and whether stopping ads prevents another unmanageable wave.
+
+## Week 26 / Day 175
+
+- Cash: $5,448,237. Individual subscribers: 138,123. Week 25 added 1,504 subscribers from 4,454 leads; cancellations 1,560. Still zero enterprise subscribed seats.
+- Despite zero ads, 766 new enterprise leads arrived, likely driven by reputation/network effects from the huge open pipeline.
+- Open-thread state before response: many threads were agent-waiting from prior offers, plus 766 fresh system-waiting leads. Important correction: do not refresh agent-waiting threads again; it may reset response timing. Only answer system-waiting leads.
+- Responded to the 766 fresh system-waiting leads only:
+  - D_E02 309 leads: A $59, B $69, C $99.
+  - D_E08 125: A $119, B $139, C $169.
+  - D_E09 114: A $79, B $99, C $129.
+  - E1 32: A $19, B $29, C $49.
+  - E2 58: A $79, B $89, C $119.
+  - E3 128: A $89, B $99, C $119.
+- Week 25 cash flow: subscription payments $3,341,935; compute -$987,524; ops -$1,022,000; capacity -$196,000; dev -$116,900; ads $0.
+- S2 issue age still high: 8,828 open, avg age 8.13 days, max 118.
+- Changes:
+  - Ads remain off.
+  - Ops raised to $167k/day total: global $35k, S2 $100k, S3 $3k, enterprise targets $29k.
+  - Dev unchanged at $16.7k/day total.
+  - Kept capacity tier 6.
+  - No new R&D.
+- Watch next week: whether older enterprise offers finally produce acceptances/counters once we stop refreshing them, enterprise lead volume with ads off, and whether higher support reduces S2 issue age/churn.
